papers.title: Reducing brassinosteroid signalling enhances grain yield in semi-dwarf wheat
papers.authors: Song L, Liu J, Cao B, Liu B, Zhang X, Chen Z, Dong C, Liu X, Zhang Z, Wang W, Chai L, Liu J, Zhu J, Cui S, He F, Peng H, Hu Z, Su Z, Guo W, Xin M, Yao Y, Yan Y, Song Y, Bai G, Sun Q, Ni Z

papers.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
papers.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowská D, et al

edges.mechanism: BR ligand binding activates TaBRI1 receptor kinase; triggers BR signalling cascade
edges.evidence_sentence: BR hormones regulate critical developmental traits in wheat through the BRI1 receptor

edges.mechanism: Activated TaBRI1 facilitates ZnF-mediated ubiquitination of TaBKI1 (BRI1 kinase inhibitor), releasing receptor
edges.evidence_sentence: TaBRI1 facilitates the ZnF-TaBKI1 conjugation in response to BR signalling

edges.mechanism: ZnF RING-type E3 ligase degrades TaBKI1 via proteasome at PM; activates BR signalling
edges.evidence_sentence: ZnF acts as a BR signalling activator to facilitate proteasomal destruction of the BR signalling repressor BRI1 kinase inhibitor 1 (TaBKI1)

edges.mechanism: TaBKI1 inhibits TaBRI1 kinase activity in absence of BR
edges.evidence_sentence: BR signalling repressor BRI1 kinase inhibitor 1 (TaBKI1)

edges.mechanism: Activated BRI1 signalling via BSK/CDG1/BSU1 inactivates BIN2 GSK3 kinase (negative regulator)
edges.evidence_sentence: In BR signaling, transphosphorylation events mediated through BSK1, CDG1 and BIN2 kinases lead to the activation of BSU1 phosphatase, which acts to dephosphorylate and inactivate BIN2, the negative regulator of BR signaling

edges.mechanism: BIN2 phosphorylates BZR1/BES1 inhibiting nuclear localization and DNA binding; active at low BR
edges.evidence_sentence: At low BR concentrations, BIN2 phosphorylates two homologous transcription factors, BZR1 and BES1/BZR2, and inhibits their nuclear localization and DNA-binding activity

edges.mechanism: Dephosphorylated nuclear BZR1/BES1 drives expression of growth and cell-elongation genes
edges.evidence_sentence: The dephosphorylated active transcription factors, BZR1 and BES1/BZR2, can then bind to specific sites on DNA and regulate expression of several genes

edges.mechanism: Functional TaBRI1 promotes stem elongation and growth; triple-mutant produces severe dwarfism
edges.evidence_sentence: Triple mutants (with premature stop codons in all three TaBRI1 homoeologues) produced severe dwarfism, malformed leaves and sterility

edges.mechanism: BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA proteins; BR-GA crosstalk; BZR1-DELLA antagonism
edges.evidence_sentence: BRs and GAs co-regulate a large number of common target genes and BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA proteins; Deletions of domains required for BZR1-DELLA interaction abolished the antagonistic effect between RGA and BZR1

edges.mechanism: ZnF-B activates BR signalling; znf loss reduces height 8.4 cm without pleiotropy
edges.evidence_sentence: znf-bb (single): Shorter (8.4 cm); Slight reduction; no pleiotropy

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Triple mutants (with premature stop codons in all three TaBRI1 homoeologues) produced severe dwarfism, malformed leaves and sterility
Missense mutants in TaBRI1-A1 proved most promising: the less severe variant was 35% shorter than the wild type
perturbations.notes: Single-homeolog missense; 35% shorter, 56% reduced flag leaf angle

perturbations.evidence_sentence: znf-bb (single): Shorter (8.4 cm); Slight reduction; no pleiotropy
perturbations.notes: ZnF-B loss reduces height 8.4cm; no yield penalty

perturbations.evidence_sentence: znf-bb rht1-bb (double): Similar to WT; Longer spikes, larger grains
perturbations.notes: Genetic linkage r-e-z deletion removes Rht-B1 and ZnF-B; height rescued but yield enhanced
